# Security Policy

## Reporting

Report suspected vulnerabilities to `contact@truststandardprotocol.com` with the
subject prefix `[SECURITY]`. Do not include secrets, private customer data,
private evidence packs, deployment runbooks, or production credentials in public
issues, discussions, or pull requests.

## In Scope

- signature verification bypass
- canonicalization mismatch
- verifier false-positive
- fail-open behavior
- TrustBadge misrepresentation
- unauthorized issuance / official-status confusion
- secret leakage

## Out of Scope

- general feature requests
- claims that an AI answer is factually wrong
- legal/compliance certification requests
- vulnerability claims outside the TSP evidence/integrity boundary

## Doctrine Boundary

TSP verifies evidence state and operational evidence integrity. TSP does not
verify truth, factual correctness, legal outcome, safety, hallucination-free
output, or EU AI Act compliance.

Public use, contribution, or forking does not grant production issuance rights,
TrustBadge authorization, or Official TSP Conformance Attestation.

Integrity verified. Factual correctness not certified.
